chief_complaint: swollen ankles and shortness of breath for 3 weeks
hpi_details: bilateral pitting oedema to mid-shin, orthopnoea (3 pillows), PND, dyspnoea on minimal exertion (walking 20 metres), no chest pain
ideas: thinks her old heart condition is acting up again
concerns: worried she'll end up in hospital again like last year
pmh: heart failure (EF 35%, diagnosed 2 years ago), atrial fibrillation, hypertension
medications: furosemide 40mg OD, bisoprolol 5mg OD, ramipril 5mg OD, warfarin (INR target 2-3)
social_hx: non-smoker, non-drinker, lives alone, daughter checks in daily

reference_soap.subjective: 72F with known HFrEF presents with 3-week worsening bilateral ankle oedema, orthopnoea (3 pillows), PND, and markedly reduced exercise tolerance. No chest pain.
reference_soap.objective: Likely fluid overload. Requires weight, JVP, lung auscultation, BNP, renal function, ECG.
reference_soap.assessment: Decompensated heart failure. DDx: medication non-compliance, dietary Na excess, worsening AF rate control, renal deterioration, new ischaemic event.
reference_soap.plan: Increase furosemide, strict fluid and sodium restriction, weigh daily, check renal function and electrolytes, consider cardiology review. Assess compliance.

learning_objectives: Systematically elicit heart failure symptoms (oedema, orthopnoea, PND, dyspnoea)
Review medication compliance and dietary history
